Statement: Number and type of patents or trademarks filed and/or received (e.g. IP data, implementation science etc.)
Number and type of trainings offered by Hub (e.g. courses, certificates, workshops, seminars, tracks, etc.)
Institutional collaboration and commitment to clinical and translational science research (e.g. number of projects and protocols, in-kind support, $ and personnel)
Number of pilot grants advancing to clinical trial proposals and/or awards
Median time to complete CTSA Hub-supported consultation and/or services (duration in days)
Number and type of measurable plans, policies or changes related to diversity, equity, and inclusion
Number and types of CTSA Hub interactions with state, local and public health entities
Qualitative data regarding how gender and racial diversity in clinical translational research can be achieved and/or what is needed
Number and types of new or ongoing collaborations with multiple CTSA Hubs and/or national consortium
Number and percent of pilot awardees overall and by relevant demographics (e.g. women and underrepresented populations )
For newly-emerging health crises requiring a rapid response: Number of CTSA-affiliated investigators publishing relevant results within X period of time (in months or years)
Relative familiarity with the term "translational science" among key indicator groups (healthcare providers, leaders of relevant community organizations and academic faculty in relevant fields)
Number and type of underrepresented populations in clinical trials tool
Number and type of CTSA Hub supported services with subsequent grants and/or publications cited
Number of different CTSA initiatives with a specific focus on greater than one of the following: quality, safety, efficiency and effectiveness of clinical research
Number, type, duration, and quality of Hub-supported community engagement services and tools
Number of datasets made discoverable as a result of Hub-supported Informatics resources
The collection of high-level success stories (e.g. novel approaches or collaborations, mitigating translational science roadblocks…)
Number and type of changes in promotion and tenure policy as the result of Hub activities
Number of Hub supported opportunities created for novel care approaches for clinical research participants relative to other regional providers
Cost per participant enrolled in NIH-supported clinical trials

Scientific interdisciplinarity as measured by number and type of Doctoral/MBA/MPH degree types, scientific areas, and/or collaborations between and support of various departments, in Hub-supported work
Return on investment (ROI): The timing and magnitude of expected total gains relative to the timing and magnitude of expected total costs
Systematic reviews and/or meta evaluation  (e.g. the Cochrane or Campbell Collaboration showing the reproducibility of results published in a discipline over a set time period )
Number and/or percent of Hub-supported Pilot projects with greater than one subsequent publication
Number of and satisfaction with formal mentors supported by the Hub
Types of research projects supported by Hub  (e.g. T1-T4, various disciplines)
Proportion of CTSA-affiliated investigators who request or receive CTSA Hub-supported materials in a language other than English
The use of Retrospective Case Studies
Number and type of active multi-center trials
Qualitative measures of Hub services quality (satisfaction, reliability, responsiveness)
Number and types of knowledge and/or skills attained by participants of trainings offered by Hubs
Number and type of new programs as a result of CTSA Hub activity (e.g. entrepreneurship, translational science.)
Number and types of collaborative research projects and collaborators within a CTSA Hub
Number and type of available infrastructure or resources for multi-site clinical trials per CTSA Hub (e.g. Access to underrepresented populations, Clinical Trials Management Systems/EHR, etc.)
Number and types of Hub collaborations with community members, advisors or partner agencies
CTSA Hub-level listing of scientific and operational innovations developed, demonstrated and disseminated
Number and type of actions generated from RPPR review within a CTSA Hub
Number of CTSA hub website page views
Number and type of award-winning innovations developed at CTSA Hubs
Tracking Hub-supported research from one step to the next on the translational spectrum (T1, T2, T3…) via operational markers (e.g. first in human, clinical trial phases, FDA approval, etc.)
Number of FDA approvals received by CTSA Hubs

Qualitative data regarding community experiences with and perceptions of research (trust, community value, equity, researcher preparedness, and indicators of successful engagement) and perceptions of optimism regarding positive health outcomes
Duration (raw number or median) in days from start to finish of IRB application (Common Metric: Could also be applied for sIRB or eSRC)
Number and type of CTSA Hub supported service consultations and services
Frequency and reach of CTSA-affiliated personnel interviewed by the media
Number of researchers served by the CTSA Hub (overall and by percent of relevant demographics e.g. women and Underrepresented Populations)
The Altmetric Attention Score (a weighted count of all of the online attention discoverable for an individual research output, including but not limited to social media, news, and policy documents)
Tracking number of new Hub personnel (e.g. tracking key personnel changes over time, turnover rate)
Number, type and percent of career impacts on participants in Hub-supported career development (e.g. promotion, subsequent funding, leadership)
Number and percent of trainees and scholars who remain engaged in research after training (Common Metric: Overall and by relevant demographics e.g. women, underrepresented populations, etc.)
Bibliometrics, general (e.g. the wide range of bibliometrics used in academia such as the H-index or the Journal impact factor)
Time from end of pilot grant to first subsequent grant and/or publication (duration in days)
Team Science readiness regarding issues such as Authorship & Credit; Contingencies & Communicating; and Conflict of Interest (e.g. Checklist published by the National Cancer Institute)
Number and type of NIH institutes or programs (outside of NCATS) using CTSA resources or CTSA developed resources
Number and type of Hub-supported faculty involved in clinical research
Number and type of community members trained  by Hubs
Number and types of CTSA Hub supported research studies (e.g. those involving health disparities or special populations)
Number (and growth in number) of patients enrolled into Hub supported trials
Measures of data quality including: performance data readability, relevance, reliability, representative, and reproducibility in Hub supported research

Proportion of positions representing individuals from underrepresented populations in research across the biomedical workforce (i.e. coordinators, technicians, analysts, not just investigators or Hub leadership)
Number of new and repeat investigators receiving CTSA Hub services
Reduced number of deferrals in CTSA Hub-supported research projects
Time to activation of new clinical trials supported by the CTSA Hub (in days)
Qualitative data regarding number and kinds of barriers faced by Hubs
Number and/or percent of Hub supported Pilot projects with greater than one subsequent grant for extramural funding
Number and percent of KL2 and/or TL1 applicants, participants and graduates (overall and by relevant demographics, e.g. women, underrepresented populations, etc.)
Number and type of Clinical and Medical Benefits (from Translational Science Benefits Model. e.g. procedures, guidelines, tools, and products)
Number and type of Economic Benefits (from Translational Science Benefits Model. e.g. commercial products, financial savings and benefits)
Number and type of Community and Public Health Benefits (from Translational Science Benefits Model. e.g. health activities, care, and promotion)
Changes made at a Hub in response to Rapid Cycle Quality Improvement (RCQI) by theme and percentage change

Quantitative measures of CTSA leadership, staff, and supporting institution and/or catchment area by demographic diversity (Underrepresented populations, gender, early-career, socioeconomic status, etc.)
Clinical trial process quality (e.g. number of audits, monitoring or biorepositories, adherence to FDA requirements)
Number of outcomes and innovations of CTSA supported and/or funded clinical research (e.g. quality, safety, efficacy, clinical and behavioral innovations and/or outcomes, IND, etc.)
Number of CTSA Hubs integrating EHR data (i.e., feasibility, recruitment)
Average time to fill clinical research professionals (CRP) positions relative to other, readily-available staff positions (duration in days)
Number of publications by CTSA Hub-affiliated authors overall, and percent by authors demographics (e.g. gender and underrepresented populations, field...)
Number and type of Policy and Legislative Benefits (from Translational Science Benefits Model. e.g. advisory activities, policies and legislation)
Proportion of phase 1 clinical trials (T1) to bench studies (T0) supported by the CTSA Hub
Number or percent of studies reaching the median accrual ratio (Common Metric: percent of participants accrued divided by percent of recruitment period to date)

Number of Hubs reporting on activities within a measurable period of time once an urgent public health need is identified for the national CTSA consortium (readiness/rapid response in days or months within specified categories e.g. research, education, training, community engagement etc.)
